sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports a fall resulting in right ankle pain, with a pop sensation and immediate discomfort. He describes the pain as a throbbing ache and rates it as 6/10. He reports being able to put some weight on it but is limping. He has iced and elevated the ankle and taken ibuprofen. He denies numbness or tingling in the foot.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals ecchymosis over the lateral malleolus and swelling. Palpation elicits tenderness in the anterior and lateral soft tissue. Capillary refill is less than 3 seconds. Dorsalis pedis pulse is strong. No laxity on anterior drawer or inversion stress. The x-ray shows no fractures or bony abnormalities.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has a right ankle sprain, likely moderate in severity based on the exam findings and patient report.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: I'm prescribing an air cast for immobilization. Prescribing crutches for 1-2 days. Advise keeping the leg elevated. Recommend continued icing 5 times a day for 20 minutes. Instruct the patient to monitor for worsening symptoms and to contact the office if needed. No doctor's note will be provided.
